title: Seele Monoliths
wikitext:
[]: Seele Monoliths             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:47 pm Why are Seele presented as monoliths? considering that they later turn up as 15 monoliths with Kaworu, they are suggested to be the angels souls rather than Seele at that point. Does that mean that Seele are already souls rather than people, considering that we never see them again.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:50 pm 1) To emphasize uniformity (ie Instrumentality) and facelessness (for the purposes of antagonism). 2) No. Kaworu's monoliths are suspected to be something different, his way of representing the Angels in the internal conversation. They are suspected to be different because in every other scene there are only 12 monoliths. 3) Seele DOES show up again. Gendo has at least one more conversation with them during EoE, and then they melt.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:56 pm Seele is always presented with the "Sound Only" monoliths. Why? I would go with what Legendary said and say that it's to represent uniformity. They wanted to achieve a perfect state of being, so they sought to acts as one, which may explain why they talk in unison. The Human Instrumentality Committee is a smaller group within Seele, and they are always "in person"/hologram.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Mon Apr 26, 2010 12:05 am God I love those monoliths. i do a series of stories based largely around conspiracy theory/the occult. Incredible pitch black comedy stuff. I use monoliths as the Illuminati's method of speaking to the main characters, two mercenaries who do their dirty work in homage.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 26, 2010 12:09 am My old and oft-loathed "15 Monoliths = Angel souls" theory is getting endorsed and I didn't have to say a thing? Feels good, man. Legendary wrote: 3) Seele DOES show up again. Gendo has at least one more conversation with them during EoE, and then they melt.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Mon Apr 26, 2010 12:22 am I meant they don't show up in human form again

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Mon Apr 26, 2010 12:23 am Reichu wrote: The monoliths are a reference to 2001: A Space Odyssey . I guess Seele have that obsession with human "evolution" going for them.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 26, 2010 12:29 am TheLobe wrote: I meant they don't show up in human form again

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Apr 26, 2010 12:50 am And they DID show up in human form again. Re: Kiel MELTING.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Apr 26, 2010 12:51 am TheLobe wrote: I meant they don't show up in human form again

[]:             Quote Post by Raiden » Fri Jun 11, 2010 10:31 am TheLobe wrote: I meant they don't show up in human form again

[]: Re: Seele Monoliths             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Jun 11, 2010 11:18 am TheLobe wrote: Why are Seele presented as monoliths?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Jun 11, 2010 11:49 am Because the monoliths look much more intimidating and set a notably darker mood than goofy-looking foreign guys sitting around a table with colored lights. Also because they don't have to animate them moving. :P

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Fri Jun 11, 2010 12:19 pm Because its cheaper to animate them that way. No need for even little mouth flaps. But seriously, them all looking the same really emphasizes the "no uniqueness" thing.

[]: Re: Seele Monoliths             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Jun 11, 2010 12:31 pm TheLobe wrote: Why are Seele presented as monoliths?

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Jun 11, 2010 2:05 pm MONEY, PEOPLE. it's always about the lack of MONEY.

[]: Re: Seele Monoliths             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Jun 11, 2010 2:18 pm Allemann wrote: Anno chose the shapes because the look complicated.

[]:             Quote Post by 0x2eleven » Sun Jun 13, 2010 3:41 am They don't want people to know who they are? One or more of them worked under Fuyutski, and perhaps they would like to remain as anonymous as possible to save face. In an episode later in the series, or was it EoE, one of the monoliths slips up and calls Fuyutski sensei . Or, maybe Anno was recycling the black monolith from Kubrick's 2001: A Space Odyssey to make some kind of statement. 2001: A Space Oddyssey:

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 13, 2010 3:48 am 0x2eleven wrote: slips up and calls Fuyutski sensei .

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Jun 13, 2010 4:16 am Reichu wrote: Only five out of those twelve Seele members ever showed their faces to us in the first place, and when they did they called themselves the "Human Instrumentality Committee" instead of "Seele".

Does that mean that Seele are already souls rather than people, considering that we never see them again.
